input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at XAML load time. Switch to **`DynamicResource`** so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the merged dictionary:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates on theme change -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code-behind like:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

## 2. Activity restarts on Android config change

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. Tell Android your app handles it itself by adding `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
// Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `UiMode` handled, the OS won't restart the activity — instead you can detect the change and swap your `ResourceDictionary` programmatically.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and doesn't update when the underlying resource changes. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android system settings, the app restarts completely. This is because Android destroys and recreates the Activity by default when configuration changes (like `uiMode`) occur. They need to handle this in their Android configuration.


I'll walk through the fixes for both issues.
